posts.content: 我生于鲁国陬邑。虽然家境贫寒，地位卑微，但我十有五而志于学。在这些繁琐的委吏与乘田差事中，我看到了礼的真谛。
posts.translation: I was born in Zou, Lu. Though my family was poor and my station low, at fifteen I set my heart on learning. Even in small tasks like managing granaries and livestock, I found the true essence of ritual.

posts.content: 少年时我浸淫六艺——礼、乐、射、御、书、数——因为我相信君子必须身心并练。
posts.translation: In my teens I buried myself in the Six Arts—ritual, music, archery, chariot-driving, calligraphy, and numbers—because I believed a gentleman must train body and mind together.

posts.content: 我赴齐国与大夫辩论——齐景公嘉纳薄赋之言，却无持久改革；我见君主往往爱口号胜过牺牲。
posts.translation: I traveled to Qi and debated ministers at court—Duke Jing welcomed ideas on lowering taxes, yet no lasting reform followed; I saw how rulers love slogans more than sacrifice.

posts.content: 终于，我在鲁国为官，从中都宰做到大司寇。我想要重塑秩序，削平三桓的羽翼。法治虽易，但德治与礼教才是治本之策。
posts.translation: At last, I held office in Lu, from Governor of Zhongdu to Minister of Justice. I sought to restore order and clip the wings of the Three Families. Rule by law is easy, but virtue and ritual are the true cures for a broken state.

posts.content: 道不行，乘桴浮于海。我离开鲁国周游列国，在卫、宋、陈、蔡之间奔走。虽累累若丧家之犬，但我心中的大同世界从未熄灭。
posts.translation: The Way does not prevail. I left Lu to wander the states—Wei, Song, Chen, and Cai. Though I am like a stray dog, the vision of a Great Harmony in my heart has never flickered out.

posts.content: 我与弟子困于陈蔡之间——粮尽仍有人问礼；我说患难才见品格。
posts.translation: We were stranded between Chen and Cai—food ran out, yet my students still asked about ritual; I told them crisis reveals character.

posts.content: 回到鲁国，我不恋高位，却向束脩而来的弟子敞开庭院——教育不该被血缘垄断。
posts.translation: Back in Lu I refused grand titles but opened my courtyard to any student who brought dried meat—education, I insisted, should not be a monopoly of bloodlines.

posts.content: 阔别多年，我终于回到了鲁国。我不愿再谈政事，只想静心删订《诗》《书》，定《礼》《乐》。弟子三千，贤人七十二，我这一生的火种，都在他们身上了。
posts.translation: After many years, I have finally returned to Lu. I no longer wish to speak of politics; I only want to edit the Odes and Documents, and settle the Rites and Music. With three thousand disciples, the sparks of my life's work live on through them.

posts.content: 我笔削《春秋》，褒贬寓于微言——让后人明白史书本身就能教人善恶。
posts.translation: I edited the Spring and Autumn annals so judgements hide in the wording—future readers would learn that history itself can teach righteousness.

posts.content: 泰山其颓乎！梁木其坏乎！哲人其萎乎！我这一辈子，从心所欲不逾矩。逝者如斯夫，不舍昼夜，但愿这世间终有仁爱流转。
posts.translation: Mount Tai crumbles! The beam breaks! The wise man withers! In my life, I have learned to follow my heart's desire without overstepping the bounds. It passes like this river, day and night, yet I hope benevolence will forever flow through this world.
